description: Double Spend / Overlap Run-Costs: Paying for existing VMware hosts and new cloud instances simultaneously if the migration stretches out.
mitigationPlan: Plan tightly coupled Move Groups to quickly evacuate and cancel entire bare-metal servers.
evidenceDetail: IBM has funding available to assist clients.

description: Proof of Value / Proof of Concept Funding: Uncertainty about cloud migration success without hands-on validation in the target environment.
mitigationPlan: Engage IBM to fund a Proof of Value or Proof of Concept exercise, validating workload compatibility and performance before committing to full migration.
evidenceDetail: IBM has funding available for proof of value/proof of concept.

description: OS & App Licensing Invalidation: Moving from BYOL on vSphere to cloud. 3rd-party apps may not be licensed for non-VMware cloud environments.
mitigationPlan: Audit all Windows/RHEL and ISV application licenses for cloud portability before migrating.

description: Business Operations Disruption: Cutover takes longer than the approved maintenance window, impacting business operations.
mitigationPlan: Establish strict Point of No Return timelines for rollback during migration weekends. Mandate dry-runs for Tier-1 apps.

description: Migration Fatigue: The core IT team is expected to handle day jobs while also executing a high-stress cloud migration project.
mitigationPlan: Backfill operational roles with contractors, or pause non-critical internal IT roadmap projects to free up capacity.

description: Security Paradigm Shift: Team is used to edge firewalls and NSX, but VPC uses Security Groups and NACLs.
mitigationPlan: Conduct mandatory hands-on enablement for the network/security teams on IBM Cloud VPC networking architecture.

description: Cloud Operating Model Gap: Team lacks Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform/Ansible) skills needed to manage VPC efficiently at scale.
mitigationPlan: Pair internal staff with cloud consultants for the first 3 migration waves. Mandate Terraform training.

description: Loss of Legacy Automation: Custom vRealize, PowerCLI, vCenter templates, vMotion-dependent workflows, SRM DR runbooks, proprietary virtual appliances, and 3rd-party VMware-integrated tooling are not usable in the new cloud environment.
mitigationPlan: Treat Tooling Migration as Phase 0. Rewrite deployment pipelines using IBM Cloud Schematics and Ansible.

description: Monitoring Blindspots: vROps and VMware-specific dashboards will no longer work, leading to visibility gaps into application health.
mitigationPlan: Deploy and map IBM Cloud Monitoring (Sysdig) agents to all workloads prior to final cutover.

description: Change Management / ITSM Breakage: ServiceNow/Jira workflows designed around VMware provisioning no longer align with cloud approvals.
mitigationPlan: Map the new VPC provisioning workflow and update the ITSM tool configurations.

description: Incompatible Backup Architecture: Tools like Veeam or Zerto may not natively back up VPC instances the same way.
mitigationPlan: Design and implement IBM Cloud Backup for VPC policies; test block-level restores before moving production data.

description: Failure to meet RPO/RTO: Previous cross-site VMware replication is gone. New architecture may require multi-zone/multi-region design.
mitigationPlan: Redesign the DR strategy using IBM Cloud cross-region replication and load balancers.

description: Image Conversion / Boot Failure: Exporting VMDK and importing as VPC Custom Image fails because cloud-init or virtio drivers are missing. Windows Server 2003 has no viable VirtIO drivers and cannot boot on VPC. Windows Server 2008/2008 R2 VirtIO drivers were dropped from current virtio-win releases (last working version: 0.1.173.9, unsupported); IBM Cloud requires virtio-win 1.9.24+ which excludes 2008. RHEL 5/6 lack cloud-init/virtio support. All are high-risk for boot failure.
mitigationPlan: For Windows Server 2012 R2+: standardize a pre-migration script to inject current virtio-win drivers. For Windows Server 2008 R2: archived virtio-win-0.1.173.9 may be manually injected but is unsupported and untested on current VPC firmware — OS upgrade to 2019/2022 strongly recommended. For Windows Server 2003: no viable drivers exist — requires OS upgrade or application re-platforming before migration.

description: Classic-to-VPC Routing Drops: Overlapping IP spaces or misconfigured Transit Gateway drops traffic between migrated and un-migrated VMs.
mitigationPlan: Map all IP subnets rigorously. Test Transit Gateway connectivity with dummy workloads first.

description: Large VMDK replication (multi-TB) extends migration windows and increases downtime risk during cutover.
mitigationPlan: Identify multi-TB VMs early. Use incremental replication (RackWare/MTV) with pre-seeding to minimize cutover window.

description: Data residency or sovereignty laws may prevent geographic transfer of workloads to available IBM Cloud VPC regions.
mitigationPlan: Map each workload's data residency requirements to available IBM Cloud MZR locations. Escalate any gaps early.

description: Regulated workloads may require certified hardware, specific encryption standards, or network segregation not yet validated in the target environment.
mitigationPlan: Catalogue compliance requirements (PCI-DSS, HIPAA, SOC2, etc.) per workload and validate IBM Cloud controls before migration.

description: VMs with no identified application owner cannot be functionally validated after migration, risking undetected failures.
mitigationPlan: Run an ownership discovery exercise before migration. Quarantine orphan VMs in the final wave with extended monitoring.

description: Client infrastructure and application staff may not be available to perform post-migration testing and fix-forward remediation, delaying wave completion and blocking subsequent waves.
mitigationPlan: Secure named testers and remediation contacts per application before each wave. Build UAT windows into the migration schedule and confirm staff availability in advance.

description: Cloud migration uses cold or warm approaches only — there is no equivalent to Cross vCenter vMotion or live migration. Every workload requires planned application downtime during cutover.
mitigationPlan: Classify workloads by downtime tolerance. Use warm migration with incremental replication to minimize cutover windows. Schedule Tier-1 apps in dedicated maintenance windows with rollback plans.
